channel.title: Just In
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026, Australian Broadcasting Corporation. All Rights Reserved.

item.title: Alleged driver charged over deaths of two girls in Perth crash
item.description: Police charge a 24-year-old man with dangerous driving causing death, a fortnight after two young girls died from the injuries they suffered when their car smashed into a tree in Viveash.
item.dc:creator: Callum Liddelow

media:group.media:description: Two girls aged three and four died in hospital nine days after their car crashed in Viveash.

item.title: Darwin company's $50 million plan to make diesel from gas condensate
item.description: Global Resource Recovery NT says production of the first diesel made from Central Australian gas condensate will leave Darwin in July.

media:group.media:description: A former biodiesel refinery is currently used as a chemical recycling facility in Darwin.

item.title: Captain of virus-hit ship praises all aboard as Australians set to return home
item.description: The MV Hondius's captain has praised the patience and kindness of his crew and guests as they disembark.

media:group.media:description: The MV Hondius has left Tenerife on its voyage to Rotterdam.

item.title: 'Relief' as Voss resigns as Carlton coach after torrid run of results
item.description: Michael Voss has left Carlton, resigning with immediate effect following a run of seven consecutive AFL defeats, with another former Carlton boss Mick Malthouse saying the Brisbane premiership-winning player will be relieved to be out of the hot seat.
item.dc:creator: Simon Smale

media:group.media:description: Michael Voss coached the Blues in 103 AFL matches.

item.title: COVID quarantine facility finally being used for disease control
item.description: Nestled away in Perth's outer fringes, this 500-bed facility was built during the COVID-19 pandemic as a quarantine centre but sat idle for years. Now, a different deadly virus will see it used for its intended purpose.
item.dc:creator: Jasmine Hughes

media:group.media:description: The Bullsbrook quarantine facility is one of three Centres for National Resilience built during COVID.

item.title: Live: ASX set to open higher as budget in focus
item.description: Australian shares are set to open slightly higher after the S&P 500 and the Nasdaq closed at record highs despite another setback in efforts to end the US-Iran war. Follow the ABC News live markets blog.
item.dc:creator: Lin Lin

media:group.media:description: The ASX 200 is slightly up.

item.title: Live: More than 70 UK Labour MPs call for Sir Keir Starmer's resignation
item.description: At least four ministerial aides have stepped down and more than 70 UK Labour MPs have publicly called for Sir Keir Starmer's resignation. Follow live.

media:group.media:description: Sir Keir Starmer is struggling to fight off demands to step down as British Prime Minister.

item.title: Family's 'most vulnerable moment' drives daughter to help hospice patients
item.description: Discussing how people want to be treated at the end of their life can be hard, but experts say planning can bring dignity and comfort.
item.dc:creator: Claudia Sullivan

media:group.media:description: Kelly Wingard says her clients are "like extended family".

item.title: 'Work to do': AFL targets Indigenous player boost
item.description: The AFL is considering a range of measures to boost the number of Indigenous players in the league following a significant decline in recent seasons.

media:group.media:description: Brad Hill is backing the AFL's bid to get more Indigenous players at the top of the sport.

item.title: 'Live at my house for a week' says mum of kids on NDIS
item.description: Regional families are anxiously waiting to see the impact of recently announced changes to the National Disability Insurance Scheme (NDIS), especially for those with autism.
item.dc:creator: Shannon Pearce and Jackson Byrne

media:group.media:description: Kirsty Johnson wants the federal health minister to understand how important her family's National Disability Insurance Scheme supports are.

item.title: Popyrin brushed aside by world number one Sinner at Italian Open
item.description: Alexei Popyrin has been outclassed in Rome, beaten 6-2 6-0 by world number one Jannik Sinner in 65 one-sided minutes in the Italian Open third round.

media:group.media:description: Alexei Popyrin was blown away by Jannik Sinner.

item.title: Apple's Tim Cook and Tesla's Elon Musk among top US CEOs to accompany Trump to China
item.description: According to a list shared by a White House official, speaking on condition of anonymity, Elon Musk and Tim Cook will be accompanied by 15 other chief executive officers when Donald Trump visits China.

media:group.media:description: Donald Trump and Xi Jinping will be meeting in China this week.

item.title: Live: Chalmers says Labor has chosen 'hard road of reform' in federal budget
item.description: Treasurer Jim Chalmers says tonight's budget will be the government's most ambitious and will feature "more than the usual amount of savings and more than the usual amount of reform". Follow live.
item.dc:creator: Joshua Boscaini

media:group.media:description: Jim Chalmers is seen from behind, walking up an outdoor flight of steps, with Parliament House in the background. ABC News: Matt Roberts

item.title: Small robots drafted to help tackle looming renewable skills shortage
item.description: Students are using tiny, programmable robots to code in a bid to encourage them to consider careers in renewable energy due to predicted skill shortages.
item.dc:creator: Jack Colantuono

media:group.media:description: The Ozobots workshops are part of a push to prepare students for the renewable energy workforce.

item.title: High Court to hear challenge over murder verdict swapped to manslaughter
item.description: The case of a Canberra man convicted over the death of his partner after she fell from the bonnet of his car will reach the High Court today, after the ACT Court of Appeal swapped the jury's verdict of murder to manslaughter.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Byrne

media:group.media:description: Lawyers for Michael O'Connell will tell the High Court that once he was acquitted of murder the court no longer had the option of a re-trial or a verdict of guilty of manslaughter.

item.title: Legal Aid NT flags more cuts to services amid ongoing funding crisis
item.description: One of two legal bodies responsible for representing vulnerable Territorians in court is considering making further cuts, six months after its services were previously slashed.
item.dc:creator: Lillian Rangiah and Joseph Hathaway-Wilson

media:group.media:description: Funding shortfalls at Legal Aid NT have seen prisoners on remand unable to access its services for at least two weeks of the past four months.

item.title: Adelaide University students critical after global data breach
item.description: Adelaide University says access to the cloud-based learning platform Canvas is back online after a data breach last week, but some students have criticised the institution's communication about the incident.
item.dc:creator: Briana Fiore and Jordanna Schriever

media:group.media:description: Adelaide University students Shannon Schmidt and Bailey Fry say the Canvas outage caused confusion among students.

item.title: Thousands spent on antennas and boosters fails to improve mobile coverage
item.description: Marten Jak lives only 10 kilometres from one of South Australia's largest regional towns. Despite spending thousands of dollars on boosters and antennas, he does not have a decent mobile phone signal.
item.dc:creator: Caroline Horn

media:group.media:description: Marten and Sarah Jak have lived in Waitpinga for decades.

item.title: Rain forecast from the tropics to Tasmania after Australia's driest April
item.description: In a welcome twist of weather variability, the first north-west cloudband of 2026 will finally develop over Australia this week, delivering a broad band of rain.
item.dc:creator: Tom Saunders

media:group.media:description: Australia is set to get a broad band of rain this week.

item.title: Wife murderer issued five-year parole period after sexual abuse of child
item.description: A man who stalked and shot his wife dead on an Adelaide street in 1992 and sexually abused a teenage girl after being released from jail has been handed a five-year parole term in South Australia's Supreme Court.
item.dc:creator: Eugene Boisvert

media:group.media:description: Colin Arthur Case killed his estranged wife in 1992 and was arrested two days later.

item.title: 'The sky isn't even my limit': Quadriplegic man invents way to pilot drone hands-free
item.description: Ever since he was a young child, Christopher Hills has wanted to fly an aircraft. Now he has made that dream a reality.
item.dc:creator: Jessica Ross

media:group.media:description: Christopher Hills has created an app that allows him to control a drone.

item.title: Man who struck puppy in the head six times issued $1,500 fine with no conviction
item.description: A member of the public who reported the abuse said the sentence made it hard to believe animal suffering was taken seriously.
item.dc:creator: Eden Gillespie

media:group.media:description: Eriz Alejo will face the Beenleigh Magistrates Court on February 13, more than one year after the alleged animal abuse.

item.title: The Australians the treasurer has in mind as he targets home ownership
item.description: Treasurer Jim Chalmers will hand down the federal budget tonight. It's expected to tackle housing inequity, but will it bring back the Great Australian Dream?
item.dc:creator: Laura Lavelle and Lexy Hamilton-Smith

media:group.media:description: Angela and Terry Oliveira are saving for a home but say the dream always seems "further and further" away.

item.title: Relief but no justice after discovery of body believed to be triple murderer
item.description: A family member of one of the victims of the Lake Cargelligo shootings says justice has not been served despite the discovery of a body believed to be that of Julian Ingram.
item.dc:creator: Sean Tarek Goodwin and Xanthe Gregory

media:group.media:description: The decomposed body, believed to be Julian Ingram, was found about 50 kilometres north-west of Lake Cargelligo.

item.title: Window for smelter offers closes, with worker torment over jobs continuing
item.description: The window for non-binding offers to purchase for the Liberty Bell Bay smelter, in Tasmania's north, has officially closed, with a union delegation taking their cause to Canberra as workers anxiously await news.
item.dc:creator: Paddy Manning

media:group.media:description: Workers at Liberty Bell Bay are seeking certainty over their jobs.
